name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-53
title: rabbit-config.py human-approval lacks idempotent message: re-running 'false' rewrites marker without notice
description: skills/rabbit-config/scripts/rabbit-config.py cmd_human_approval lines 171-174: when action == 'false', it unconditionally writes the marker (overwriting any existing content silently). Spec Inv 55 requires idempotence: 're-invoking when the marker already exists is a no-op exit 0 with the same confirmation'. The current implementation prints 'Human-approval gate DISABLED (false). Marker written: ...' even when the marker already existed — it actually overwrites. Test t14 happens to pass because content stays 'session', but if a user manually wrote a different content the script silently destroys it. Add an existence check + 'already disabled' messaging, or document that 'session' is always overwritten.

history.note: wave-4 cleanup; fixed in e10610a (impl) and 91f2c68 (tests)
